Cl_interp is a command in video games that stands for interpolation. It decides the interval between the current rendering time and the current client-side, which is how often your client gets updated by the server. The default interpolation period on Source is 100-milliseconds1. Cl_interp and Cl_interp_ratio commands allow you to control the time elapsed before your movements and gunshots are recorded, so you can shorten this time and fire your weapons
